LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ZACHARY ANAYA'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH FCA US LLC'S RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENT REQUEST NOS. 29, 30, 31, 32 AND 33: THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FCA US LLC'S APPEARANCE AT DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART AS SET FORTH IN THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION. BY DECEMBER 22, 2021, FCA SHALL PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS AND SHALL SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL VERIFIED RESPONSES CONFIRMING THAT ALL RESPONSIVE, NON-PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED. DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WITH THE CONTACT INFORMATION FOR PRECIOUS BROWN AND RICK NGUYEN SO THAT PLAINTIFF MAY SERVE THEM WITH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Apr-11-2022 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-23-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Fca Us Llcs Response To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Document Request Nos. 10, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28
Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Off Calendar

Defendant Fca Us Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-23-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant FCA US LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied.

Plaintiff sufficiently pleads a breach of the express warranty under the Song Beverly Act. Plaintiff must allege that: (1) he/she bought a "new motor vehicle" from FCA (2) that FCA gave a written warranty, (3) that the vehicle had defects, covered by the warranty, that substantially impaired its use, value or safety, (4) that the plaintiff delivered the vehicle to FCA or their authorized repair facility to repair the defect, (5) that FCA failed to repair the vehicle to match the written warranty after a reasonable number of opportunities to do so, and (6) FCA did not replace or buy back the vehicle. (See Civil Code sec. 1793.2(d); CACI No. 3201.)

Here, plaintiff sufficiently alleges "presentation" under CACI 3202. First, plaintiff alleges that defendant had at least two repair opportunities, which meets the statutory requirement. (Complaint, pars. 16, 19; Silvio v. Ford Motor Co. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1205, 1208-1209 [more than one attempt is necessary to meet Civil Code sec. 1793.2(d)(2)].) A factual issue remains whether the ticking transmission problem and the fire transmission problem were in fact the same defect. Second, plaintiff alleges that the fire transmission problem destroyed the vehicle. (Complaint, par. 31.) Common sense and the express language of CACI 3202 indicate that a consumer need not provide a product for repair after it has been destroyed. (See also Bishop v. Hyundai Motor America (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 750 [Song Beverly Act claim viable where vehicle was destroyed and there was no evidence of multiple presentations]; Gomez v. Volkswagen of America, Inc. (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 921 [same].)

Defendant's argument regarding whether plaintiff can recover for damage to the trailer merely pertains to the amount of damages that plaintiff can recover. That argument fails to show that plaintiff has no claim since the destroyed truck is clearly a warranted product. Defendant's argument regarding civil penalties is also a non-starter. Again, that issue merely pertains to one component of what plaintiff can recover. (See Civil Code sec. 1794(c)["If the buyer establishes that the failure to comply was willful, the judgment may include, in addition to the amounts recovered under subdivision (a), a civil penalty"].) In any event, the complaint sufficiently alleges that defendant acted willfully. (Complaint, pars. 35-37.)

Plaintiff also pleads a breach of the implied warranty of merchantability. (CACI No. 1231; Civ. Code. Sec. 1791.1.) The trier of fact could reasonably conclude that a truck that violently explodes into flames was not "fit for the ordinary purposes for which such goods are used." (Civil Code sec. 1791.1(a)(2).) Defendant fails to show that the claim is time-barred. (See Civil Code sec. 1795.6 [tolling]; Mexia v. Rinker Boat Co. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1297, 1301 ["the plain language of the statute, particularly in light of the consumer protection policies supporting the Song-Beverly Act, make clear that the statute merely creates a limited, prospective duration for the implied warranty of merchantability; it does not create a deadline for discovering latent defects or for giving notice to the seller."]; Ehrlich v. BMW of N. Am., LLC (C.D. Cal. 2010) 801 F.Supp.2d 908, 924 ["Mexia directly addressed and rejected the precise argument BMW makes here, holding that, so long as a latent defect existed within the one-year period, its subsequent discovery beyond that time did not defeat an implied warranty claim."].) Plaintiff alleges a latent defect. (Complaint, par. 48.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Fca Us Llcs Response To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Document Request Nos. 29, 30, 31, 32 And 33
Off Calendar Dec-06-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Elliot Conn, Esq. (415.417.2780; elliot@connlawpc.com) of Conn Law, PC for Plaintiff.
Attorney appearing for Defendant will be:
James Mayo, (916.780.4050; jmayo@uswlaw.com) of Universal & Shannon LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ZACHARY ANAYA'S Motion To Compel Compliance With FCA US LLC's Response To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Document Request Nos. 29, 30, 31, 32 And 33:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel FCA US LLC's appearance at deposition and production of documents is granted in part as set forth in the report and recommendation. By December 22, 2021, FCA shall produce all responsive documents and shall serve supplemental verified responses confirming that all responsive, non-privileged documents have been produced. Defendant shall provide Plaintiff's counsel with the contact information for Precious Brown and Rick Nguyen so that Plaintiff may serve them with deposition subpoenas.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
